name: Responsys (Actions)
description: Send Profile List Member and Profile Extension Table data to Responsys.

segmentWriteKey.description: Optionally forward Responses from Segment's requests to Responsys to a Segment Source.
segmentWriteKey.label: Segment Source WriteKey

segmentWriteKeyRegion.description: Segment Region to forward responses from Responsys to. Segment Source WriteKey must also be populated
segmentWriteKeyRegion.label: Segment WriteKey Region

baseUrl.description: Responsys endpoint URL. Refer to Responsys documentation for more details. Must start with 'HTTPS://'. See [Responsys docs](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/marketing/responsys-develop/API/GetStarted/Authentication/auth-endpoints-rest.htm).
baseUrl.label: Responsys endpoint URL

defaultFolderName.description: Name of the folder where the Profile Extension Table is located.
defaultFolderName.label: Default Folder Name

profileListName.description: Name of the Profile Extension Table's Contact List.
profileListName.label: Default Profile List Name

profileExtensionTable.description: Default Profile Extension Table (PET) Name. Required if using the "Send Custom Traits" Action. Can be overridden in the mapping.
profileExtensionTable.label: Default PET Name

insertOnNoMatch.description: Indicates what should be done for records where a match is not found.
insertOnNoMatch.label: Insert On No Match

matchColumnName1.description:
First match column for determining whether an insert or update should occur.
                      A trailing underscore (_) is added to the match column name at the time of request 
                      to Responsys. 
                      This aligns with Responsys’ naming conventions for match columns.
matchColumnName1.label: First Column Match

matchColumnName2.description:
Second match column for determining whether an insert or update should occur.
                      A trailing underscore (_) is added to the match column name at the time of request 
                      to Responsys. 
                      This aligns with Responsys’ naming conventions for match columns.
matchColumnName2.label: Second Column Match

updateOnMatch.description: Controls how the existing record should be updated. Defaults to Replace All.
updateOnMatch.label: Update On Match

textValue.description: Value of incoming preferred email format data. For example, 'T' may represent a preference for Text formatted email.
textValue.label: Text Value

matchOperator.description: Operator to join match column names.
matchOperator.label: Match Operator

optoutValue.description: Value of incoming opt-out status data that represents an optout status. For example, 'O' may represent an opt-out status.
optoutValue.label: Optout Value

rejectRecordIfChannelEmpty.description: String containing comma-separated channel codes that if specified will result in record rejection when the channel address field is null. See [Responsys API docs](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/marketing/responsys-rest-api/op-rest-api-v1.3-lists-listname-members-post.html)
rejectRecordIfChannelEmpty.label: Reject Record If Channel Empty

defaultPermissionStatus.description: This value must be specified as either OPTIN or OPTOUT. Defaults to OPTOUT. Can be overridden in the mapping.
defaultPermissionStatus.label: Default Permission Status

htmlValue.description: Value of incoming preferred email format data. For example, 'H' may represent a preference for HTML formatted email.
htmlValue.label: Preferred Email Format

optinValue.description: Value of incoming opt-in status data that represents an opt-in status. For example, 'I' may represent an opt-in status.
optinValue.label: Optin Value

oauth.description: Authorizes Segment to OAuth to the Destination API
oauth.label: OAuth

access-token.description: The (legacy) access token provided by Destination API after the OAuth handshake.

access_token.description: The access token provided by Destination API after the OAuth handshake.

appId.description: The App ID, retrieved via Destination API post-auth.

appName.description: The authorized user App, retrieved via Destination API on settings view load.

createdBy.description: Email address of segment user who connected OAuth.

displayName.description: The authorized user, retrieved via Destination API on settings view load.

refresh_token.description: The refresh token provided by Destination API after the OAuth handshake.

actions.name: Send Audience
actions.description: Send Engage Audience to a Profile Extension Table in Responsys

fields.label: Recipient Data
fields.description: Record data that represents field names and corresponding values for each profile.

userData.title: Recipient Data
userData.description: Record data that represents field names and corresponding values for each profile.

fields.label: Segment Audience Key
fields.description: A unique identifier assigned to a specific audience in Segment.

computation_key.title: Segment Audience Key
computation_key.description: A unique identifier assigned to a specific audience in Segment.

fields.label: Traits or Properties
fields.description: Hidden field used to access traits or properties objects from Engage payloads.

traits_or_props.title: Traits or Properties
traits_or_props.description: Hidden field used to access traits or properties objects from Engage payloads.

fields.label: Segment Audience Computation Class
fields.description: Hidden field used to verify that the payload is generated by an Audience. Payloads not containing computation_class = 'audience' or 'journey_step' will be dropped before the perform() fuction call.

computation_class.title: Segment Audience Computation Class
computation_class.description: Hidden field used to verify that the payload is generated by an Audience. Payloads not containing computation_class = 'audience' or 'journey_step' will be dropped before the perform() fuction call.

fields.label: Use Responsys Async API
fields.description: Once enabled, Segment will collect events into batches of 200 before sending to Responsys.

enable_batching.title: Use Responsys Async API
enable_batching.description: Once enabled, Segment will collect events into batches of 200 before sending to Responsys.

fields.label: Batch Size
fields.description: Maximum number of events to include in each batch. Actual batch sizes may be lower.

batch_size.title: Batch Size
batch_size.description: Maximum number of events to include in each batch. Actual batch sizes may be lower.

fields.label: Stringify Recipient Data
fields.description: If true, all Recipient data will be converted to strings before being sent to Responsys.

stringify.title: Stringify Recipient Data
stringify.description: If true, all Recipient data will be converted to strings before being sent to Responsys.

fields.label: Timestamp
fields.description: The timestamp of when the event occurred.

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: The timestamp of when the event occurred.

fields.label: Delay (seconds)
fields.description:
A delay of the selected seconds will be added before retrying a failed request.
                    Max delay allowed is 600 secs (10 mins). The default is 0 seconds.

retry.title: Delay (seconds)
retry.description:
A delay of the selected seconds will be added before retrying a failed request.
                    Max delay allowed is 600 secs (10 mins). The default is 0 seconds.

actions.name: Send Audience as PET (Profile Extension Table)
actions.description: Send Engage Audience to a separate, newly created Profile Extension Table in Responsys

fields.label: Recipient Data
fields.description: Record data that represents field names and corresponding values for each profile.

userData.title: Recipient Data
userData.description: Record data that represents field names and corresponding values for each profile.

RIID_.title: Recipient ID
RIID_.description: Recipient ID (RIID). RIID is required if Email Address and Customer ID are empty. Only use it if the corresponding profile already exists in Responsys.

fields.label: Folder Name
fields.description: The name of the folder where the new Profile Extension Table will be created. Overrides the default folder name in Settings.

folder_name.title: Folder Name
folder_name.description: The name of the folder where the new Profile Extension Table will be created. Overrides the default folder name in Settings.

fields.label: Profile Extension Table Name
fields.description: The PET (Profile Extension Table) name. The default value is the audience key in Segment.

pet_name.title: Profile Extension Table Name
pet_name.description: The PET (Profile Extension Table) name. The default value is the audience key in Segment.

fields.label: Segment Audience Key
fields.description: A unique identifier assigned to a specific audience in Segment.

computation_key.title: Segment Audience Key
computation_key.description: A unique identifier assigned to a specific audience in Segment.

fields.label: Traits or Properties
fields.description: Hidden field used to access traits or properties objects from Engage payloads.

traits_or_props.title: Traits or Properties
traits_or_props.description: Hidden field used to access traits or properties objects from Engage payloads.

fields.label: Timestamp
fields.description: The timestamp of when the event occurred.

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: The timestamp of when the event occurred.

fields.label: Delay (seconds)
fields.description:
A delay of the selected seconds will be added before retrying a failed request.
                Max delay allowed is 600 secs (10 mins). The default is 0 seconds.

retry.title: Delay (seconds)
retry.description:
A delay of the selected seconds will be added before retrying a failed request.
                Max delay allowed is 600 secs (10 mins). The default is 0 seconds.

fields.label: Default Permission Status
fields.description: This value must be specified as either OPTIN or OPTOUT. It defaults to the value defined in this destination settings.

default_permission_status.title: Default Permission Status
default_permission_status.description: This value must be specified as either OPTIN or OPTOUT. It defaults to the value defined in this destination settings.

fields.label: Enable Batching?
fields.description: When enabled, Segment will send events in batches.
